title: End of module: Waves and Optics

questions.text: A wave has frequency $50\,\text{Hz}$ and wavelength $4\,\text{m}$. Find its speed.
questions.explanation: Using the wave equation $v = f\lambda$: $v = 50 \times 4 = 200\,\text{m s}^{-1}$.

questions.text: Sound travels at $340\,\text{m s}^{-1}$ in air. Find the wavelength of a sound wave of frequency $680\,\text{Hz}$.
In which type of wave does the oscillation occur perpendicular to the direction of energy transfer?
questions.explanation: By definition, a transverse wave oscillates perpendicular to its direction of travel (e.g. light, water surface waves).

questions.text: Which of the following waves can be polarised?
choices: A longitudinal wave on a spring
questions.explanation: Only transverse waves can be polarised, since polarisation restricts the oscillation to one plane perpendicular to the direction of travel. Light and radio waves are both transverse electromagnetic waves; sound and longitudinal spring waves have no perpendicular oscillation to restrict.

questions.text: A string of length $0.80\,\text{m}$, fixed at both ends, vibrates in its second harmonic ($n = 2$). Find the wavelength.
questions.explanation: For a string fixed at both ends, $L = n\lambda_n/2$, so $\lambda_2 = 2L/n = (2 \times 0.80)/2 = 0.80\,\text{m}$.

questions.text: The wave speed on the string in the previous question is $40\,\text{m s}^{-1}$. Find the frequency of the second harmonic.
At a node in a stationary (standing) wave, the amplitude of oscillation is…
questions.explanation: A node is a point of permanent zero displacement — destructive superposition occurs there at all times.

questions.text: Two wave sources with the same frequency and a constant (unchanging) phase difference are said to be… (one word)
questions.explanation: Sources with a constant phase difference are **coherent** — coherence is required for a stable interference pattern to be observed.

questions.text: In a double-slit experiment, the slit separation is $0.25\,\text{mm}$, the screen is $2.4\,\text{m}$ away, and the fringe spacing is $5.28\,\text{mm}$. Find the wavelength of the light, in metres.
A diffraction grating has $300$ lines per millimetre. Find the grating spacing $d$, in metres.
Using the grating from the previous question ($d = 3.33\times10^{-6}\,\text{m}$) and light of wavelength $600\,\text{nm}$, find $\sin\theta$ for the first-order ($n=1$) maximum.
Total internal reflection can only occur when light travels…
choices: From a less dense medium to a more dense medium
From a more dense medium to a less dense medium
At normal incidence to the boundary
questions.explanation: TIR requires light to travel from a denser medium towards a less dense one (e.g. glass to air), at an angle of incidence greater than the critical angle.

questions.text: A glass block has a refractive index of $1.50$ relative to air. Find its critical angle, in degrees.
Light travels from air ($n_1 = 1.00$) into water ($n_2 = 1.33$) at an angle of incidence of $40^\circ$. Find the angle of refraction, in degrees.
